raw_consumer_statement:
This complaint is a follow-up to CFPB XXXX # XXXX. 

The companys response is incomplete and does not properly reconcile the account. The transactions in question were exchanges of defective merchandise, not new purchases. 

The company treated replacement items as new charges while failing to properly offset the corresponding returns. Specifically, the {$770.00} refunded item ( confirmed by Best Buy ) was not correctly applied in the account reconciliation. 

When properly accounting for exchanges ( original purchase minus returned defective item plus replacement ), the net balance should be XXXX or a credit. Instead, the companys method double-counts charges and creates an artificial balance. 

Additionally, the response does not address missing rewards/points or price match adjustments tied to the original purchase. 

I am requesting a full transactional reconciliation of all exchange activity, including : Proper netting of purchases and returns Application of all refunds Restoration of rewards/points and price adjustments Reversal of all interest charges related to the incorrect balance Correction of the account to reflect the accurate balance ( zero or credit ) The prior response confirms the transactions occurred but fails to apply them correctly.
